operating_state: Net revenues declined 27% to $12.93 billion driven by significant losses in equity and debt investments within Asset Management and substantially lower equity underwriting revenues reflecting industry-wide activity declines. Global Markets revenues remained stable with higher FICC revenues from currencies and commodities offsetting lower equities and credit products amid elevated client activity during macroeconomic volatility. Consumer & Wealth Management revenues increased driven by growth in wealth management and consumer banking with higher average assets under supervision and increased credit card balances.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased 18% primarily due to lower compensation and benefits expenses reflecting reduced operating performance versus the prior year. The efficiency ratio deteriorated to 59.7% from 53.3% as expense reductions lagged revenue declines. Provision for credit losses increased to $561 million from a $70 million benefit, primarily reflecting credit card portfolio growth and macroeconomic concerns.
material_events: Completed acquisitions of GreenSky in Consumer banking during March and NN Investment Partners in Asset Management during April, expanding point-of-sale financing capabilities and accelerating strategy for durable returns. Returned $1.21 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. Goldman Sachs initiated the process of winding down operations in Russia following the invasion of Ukraine.
macro_exposures: The Russian invasion resulted in approximately $300 million in net losses with total credit exposure to Russia of $260 million as of March. Broad market volatility from geopolitical tensions, inflation pressures, and rising commodity prices contributed to equity price declines and credit spread widening. Average daily VaR increased to $98 million from $83 million driven by higher volatility across commodities, interest rates, and currencies. The firm's G-SIB surcharge increased from 2.5% to 3.0% effective 2023.
forward_outlook: Management established medium-term targets of achieving 14-16% ROE and 15-17% ROTE with an efficiency ratio of approximately 60% over three years. Announced target to achieve annual management and other fees exceeding $10 billion including more than $2 billion from alternative assets by 2024. Strategic objective for $225 billion in gross alternative investment inflows from 2020 through 2024 with $131 billion raised through first quarter.
uncertainty_profile: Economic outlook remains uncertain reflecting potential continuation of Russia-Ukraine conflict, persistent inflation and supply chain complications, and ongoing COVID-19 effects. Under adverse economic scenarios, allowance for credit losses would increase by approximately $1.3 billion. LIBOR transition risk persists with significant derivatives and debt instruments requiring rate conversion.
